nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal sandy areas, rubble zones, and seagrass-bed margins in warm Atlantic waters

avoidWith: tanks with no light schedule control

summary: Mega Fern Caulerpa (Caulerpa ashmeadii) is a large, feathery green macroalgae native to the Atlantic and Caribbean, valued as a striking display specimen. It tends to grow more slowly than most Caulerpa species. Like all Caulerpa, it releases allelopathic compounds and is banned for sale in California.
careNotes:
Mega Fern Caulerpa is a siphonous green macroalgae bearing large, fern-like fronds considerably bigger than those of the closely related Feather Caulerpa (C. sertularioides [sw-algae-035]). The fronds grow from a creeping stolon — a horizontal stem that anchors into sand, rubble, or rockwork via root-like structures called rhizoids. The species tends to grow more slowly than other Caulerpa and is primarily kept as a display specimen, though it can also be used in a refugium (a separate chamber or tank dedicated to growing algae for nutrient export). This species is uncommon in the hobby trade and has been available only through a small number of specialty online retailers.

Cuttings are typically sold in 2–3" (2–3 blade) and 5"+ (4+ blade) grades. The notably large frond size is the main ornamental appeal of this species compared to smaller fern-form Caulerpa. Specimens that arrive looking pale or washed out generally recover their color within a few hours under appropriate lighting.

ALLELOPATHY: Like all Caulerpa species, C. ashmeadii is expected to release secondary metabolites — including caulerpin and caulerpenyne — into the water column. These compounds have been shown to inhibit or stress nearby corals and competing algae. Physical separation from sensitive SPS corals (small-polyp stony corals, which are among the most demanding reef inhabitants) is generally advised. Running activated carbon is commonly recommended by experienced keepers to help reduce allelopathic load. Related Caulerpa entries such as [sw-algae-004] and [sw-algae-035] cover additional genus-level considerations.

GOING SEXUAL / REPRODUCTIVE CRASH: Under stress — particularly nutrient crashes, disrupted photoperiod, or rapid temperature swings — Caulerpa species can undergo a process called holocarpy, in which the entire plant converts to reproductive cells and effectively dies. This releases organic matter and gametes into the water column, posing a severe acute water-quality risk (ammonia spike, oxygen depletion) capable of crashing a reef tank. Mitigations: run refugium lighting on a 24-hour cycle; harvest or prune regularly to keep the colony in active vegetative growth; maintain stable moderate nutrient levels; remove any specimen showing white or translucent fronds, or visible turbidity from gamete release, immediately.

INVASIVE CONCERN: The genus Caulerpa includes several documented marine invasive species. Captive specimens must never be released into natural waterways. This species is banned for sale in California and cannot be shipped there.
breedingNotes: Like other Caulerpa species, Mega Fern Caulerpa propagates primarily through fragmentation. Separated frond or stolon pieces can establish as independent plants. In captivity, the primary growth mode is vegetative tip growth along the stolon, which can be encouraged by periodic pruning.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole known hobby trade source for this species at time of research. Product page confirms scientific name (Caulerpa ashmeadii), display suitability, large fern-like frond morphology, slower growth rate relative to other Caulerpa, California ban, and typical arrival bleaching. Water parameters and detailed husbandry extrapolated from genus-level Caulerpa standards.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Caulerpa (genus)
additional.notes: Confirms C. ashmeadii Harvey, 1858 as a recognized species in the genus list. Confirms Family Caulerpaceae, Order Bryopsidales. Notes genus-level secondary metabolites (caulerpin, caulerpenyne) and fast growth / nitrate-absorbing characteristics.

additional.name: AquaMate cross-reference: Caulerpa prolifera (sw-algae-004)
additional.notes: Sister Atlantic Caulerpa species used to corroborate standard reef water parameters (temp 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 1.023–1.025, kH 8–12) and Caulerpa-specific caveats (allelopathy, reproductive crash, invasive concern). Parameters applied to this entry as genus-level extrapolation.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
